ops.title: agent-browser provider plugin protocol (v1) + cookiesync bridge routing
ops.body:
Reference for wiring the cookiesync CDP bridge into agent-browser (Tracks 3a `ab bridge` mode + 3b native `--provider cookiesync`). Verified against installed agent-browser 0.31.2 (Rust binary, `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/agent-browser/0.31.2/...`) cross-checked with source `/private/tmp/agent-browser-src` (0.31.1, no protocol drift).

## `--provider <name>` resolution (NOT PATH-based)
Config registry only: `AGENT_BROWSER_PLUGINS` env (JSON array, replaces file discovery) OR `~/.agent-browser/config.json` (global) merged with `./agent-browser.json` (project, appended â†’ last-match-by-name wins). Entry shape `{name, command, args, capabilities, source}`. Must declare capability `"browser.provider"` or invocation errors. `--provider cookiesync` matches entry name=="cookiesync". `plugin add` derives name by stripping `agent-browser-plugin-`/`plugin-` prefix.

## Protocol `agent-browser.plugin.v1` â€” exec-once, no handshake
Fresh process per call; agent-browser writes ONE JSON request to stdin then closes it, reads ALL stdout as one JSON object, expects exit 0. `success!=true` / wrong `protocol` / non-JSON = hard error. stderr discarded.
- **browser.launch** (cap `browser.provider`, 60s, fired by `--provider`): request `{"protocol":"agent-browser.plugin.v1","type":"browser.launch","capability":"browser.provider","request":{"provider":"cookiesync","session":"default","launchOptions":{"headed":false,"engine":"chrome","userAgent":null,"colorScheme":null}}}`. Response `{"protocol":"agent-browser.plugin.v1","success":true,"browser":{"cdpUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:<port>/<token-path>","directPage":false,"cleanup":{<opaque>}}}`. `cdpUrl` aliases: wsUrl/connectUrl/cdp_url; wrapper key `browser` or `data`. `directPage:false` (default) â†’ agent-browser runs normal discover_and_attach_targets() (correct for our multi-target Chrome â€” it finds the sole OTR page). `cleanup` stored verbatim, echoed back on close.
- **browser.close** (15s, on connect-failure/close): request == the exact `cleanup` value; reply `{"protocol":"agent-browser.plugin.v1","success":true}`. â†’ call `cookiesync bridge stop` here.
- **plugin.manifest** (optional, for `plugin add` auto-discovery): reply `{...,"success":true,"manifest":{"name":"cookiesync","capabilities":["browser.provider"],"description":"..."}}`.

## Attach semantics (central risk â€” RESOLVED)
`resolve_cdp_url` (`browser.rs:1681`): a `ws://`/`wss://` cdpUrl is used BYTE-FOR-BYTE (scheme/host/port/path/query preserved), handed straight to the CDP websocket â€” NO `/json/version` probe. `/json/version` discovery only fires for a bare numeric port or `http(s)://host:port/nonempty-path`. So our token-path-prefix ws URL (which 403s a bare `/json/version`) works exactly as designed via the provider path. Same for `--cdp '<full ws url>'`.

## Design decision (3b): in-binary subcommand, not a separate exe
Implement the provider as `cookiesync bridge plugin` (hidden subcommand) speaking the stdio protocol, reusing bridge open/stop directly. Register `{"name":"cookiesync","command":"cookiesync","args":["bridge","plugin"],"capabilities":["browser.provider"]}`. One binary, no separate install/version surface.

## `ab` router (3a) â€” cc-skills bin/ab (bash, 245 lines)
Routing = `force_local` (from literal `--local` in argv) + `BROWSERBASE_API_KEY` presence; no AB_MODE env. Browserbase path is the precedent: fetch connectUrl â†’ `agent-browser --session "$SLUG" connect "$url"` â†’ fall through to `exec agent-browser --session "$SLUG" "${args[@]}"`. Insertion point for a `bridge` branch: alongside the `mode`/`is_close` special-casing (ab:73-90). Teardown â†’ `cookiesync bridge stop`, ideally hooked into the existing exit trap (ab:171-196). No cookiesync/bridge/--cdp refs exist in cc-skills yet (greenfield).

## `cookiesync bridge open` output (internal/cli/bridge.go)
Prints 4 human lines (printBridgeReady ~L201): `agent-browser connect '<url>'`, `export AGENT_BROWSER_CDP='<url>'`, `chromium.connectOverCDP('<url>')`. NO `--json` today (bridgeOpenResult struct IS json-tagged for RPC). ACTION: add `--json` to `bridge open`/`ls`/`stop` matching internal/cli/browser.go's convention, so `ab` does `jq -r .url` instead of scraping. Client capability persists at `~/.config/cookiesync/bridge/caps/<sha256(host:browser:profile)>`.</body>
</invoke>
